data.description:
# Tier-2: native cross-platform scheduler registration for the enrichment drain

## Context
Follow-on to the cross-ticket overlap epic (only-crave-art). v1 ships Tier 1 only (opportunistic detached drain). Tier 2 adds a `rebar maintenance start`-style command that registers a recurring drain with the native platform scheduler, for repos that are not actively touched (Tier 1 goes stale when nobody runs rebar).

Prior art: `git maintenance start` abstracts over four schedulers behind `--scheduler=auto|crontab|systemd-timer|launchctl|schtasks` — launchd on macOS (so credentials/API keys resolve — the documented reason to avoid cron there), schtasks on Windows, systemd-timer-else-cron on Linux. Register hourly/daily/weekly timers, random minute per client (avoid fleet stampede on the shared remote), full binary path baked in, and split `register` (config-only) from `start` (installs timers).

## Why deferred
Prove the daemon-free Tier-1 model works first. Tier 2 is the most code (four-backend abstraction) for the narrowest case (dormant repos).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rebar maintenance start` registers a recurring enrichment drain via the platform-appropriate scheduler (launchd/schtasks/systemd-timer/cron, `auto`-selected).
- [ ] hourly/daily/weekly timers, random minute per client, absolute binary path baked in.
- [ ] `register` (config-only) vs `start` (installs timers) are separate; a `stop`/`unregister` cleanly removes them.
- [ ] macOS uses launchd (not cron) so the drain can resolve the API key.
data.title: Tier-2: native cross-platform scheduler registration for the enrichment drain
